relative_path: 4. Meetings & Updates/Presentations/2025/12.5.25-TLS analysis H&E and IHC, summary.pptx
full_text:
TLS analysis H&E and IHC, summary

Hanna Elomaa 12.5.2025

Overview of the experiments

Identification of Lagg+/TLS+ tumors visually from H&E-stained whole slide images
Automated cell segmentation model for H&E-stained Lagg+/TLS+ tumors (Oskari Lehtonen), N=182
Selecting benign omentum samples to analyze milky spots, N=10 (estimate)
One-cycle CycIF-staining for Lagg+/TLS+ tumors and benign omentum samples, N=182

Cohort (all slides included)

Full cohort → 385 slides analyzed from 299 patients**
343 tumors → 42 patients have > 1 tumors
38 tumors have several consecutive blocks 
Lagg+/TLS+ → 182 slides: 164 tumors / 161 patients
 Lagg-/TLS- → 203 slides: 189 tumors / 164 patients

** sum for patients TLS+ and TLS- does not match with full cohort as some patients had both TLS+ and TLS- section from the same tumor block

Final cohort (1 slide/tumor, for CycIF and stat. analyses)

Full cohort → 343 slides/tumors analyzed from 299 patients
42 patients have > 1 tumors
treatment distribution: i=106, p=229, r=8
tissue distribution: Ome 214 (62%), Adn 59 (17%), Ova 41 (12%), Per 10 (3%), other=19

Lagg+/TLS+ → 164 slides/tumors analyzed from 161 patients
treatment distribution: i=73, p=90, r=1
tissue distribution: Ome 144 (88%), Adn 7 (4%), Ova 4 (2%), Per 3 (2%), other 6

Lagg-/TLS- → 179 slides/tumors analyzed from 158 patients
treatment distribution: i=33, p=139, r=7
tissue distribution: Ome 70 (39%), Adn 52 (29%), Ova 37 (21%), Per 7 (4%), Tub 4 (2%), other 9

Negative omentum controls: 10 samples

Patient counts for analyzed H&E slides and clinical data

Clinical data for 547 patients
H&E-slides available and analyzed now for 299 patients
Samples found on clinical data but H&E is missing: N=248
biobank samples (“no sample for ONCOSYS, but consented”): N=143 
PARP cohort “PARP cohort, no sample for ONCOSYS”: N=46
ascites samples etc.: N=63

Distribution of histologies (tumor level data, N=343)

eoc = ca endometrioides ovarii G3
ccc = ca clarocellulare

Histologies of Lagg/TLS+ tumors

Histologies of Lagg-/TLS- tumors

Treatment

Histologies of Lagg/TLS+ tumors

Histologies of Lagg-/TLS- tumors

Visual identification of Lagg and TLS from H&E-slides

Rectangle annotations around all Lagg and TLS
Visual categorization of Lagg/TLS into 9 categories (localization&maturation)
site: adipose tissue, stromal tissue, interface of stroma and adipose
maturation: Lagg, immature TLS, mature TLS

Outcome
> identification of Lagg+/TLS+ tumors
> visually determined categorization of maturation and localization

Tumor purity % (from patient level data, N=299)

25 tumors with tumor purity 0%
12 tumors with tumor purity 1%

2. Cell segmentation model

Detection and classification of cells (tumor cells and immune cells) and tumor compartments (tumor and stroma [and adipose tissue?]) from H&E slides


> Possibility to detect Lagg/TLS from H&Es based on cell number and area
> Not possible to detect germinal centers, may be challenging to separate macrophages from T cells
> May not be offer more information than CycIF but interesting to test

3. Benign omentum samples for negative controls

CycIF panel including:
CD3/CD4/CD8 → T cells
CD20 → B cells
IBA-1 → macrophages
Ki-67 → proliferating cells

Machine learning-based image analysis for classification of cells and possibly tissue compartments

> Detailed characterization of maturation stages of Lagg/TLS
> Area and cell count of Lagg and TLS, x,y-coordinates within TME, possibly tissue localization
> Immune cell detection inside and outside of Lagg/TLS

4. CycIF staining to identify Lagg/TLS
